title: CI/CD Infrastructure & Environment Architecture Audit
scope: All repositories — preference-frontend, planner-frontend, workforce-frontend, supabase-receptor, backend/receptor-planner, backend/match-backend

findings.description: planner-frontend and workforce-frontend CI jobs do not export ANON_JWT (legacy JWT form of ANON_KEY). Any test calling signInWithPassword() will receive an sb_publishable_* key, which is rejected by GoTrue. The October 1 2025 Supabase key format migration deadline has already passed. globalSetup files in these repos have not been audited for JWT reliance.

tasks.description: Audit globalSetup.ts in planner-frontend for any signInWithPassword() calls and confirm whether ANON_JWT is required.
Audit globalSetup.ts in workforce-frontend for any signInWithPassword() calls and confirm whether ANON_JWT is required.
Add ANON_JWT export to planner-frontend ci.yml Start Supabase step, mirroring the preference-frontend pattern (grep '^ANON_KEY=' | sed + tr -d quotes).
Add ANON_JWT export to workforce-frontend ci.yml Start Supabase step, mirroring the preference-frontend pattern.

findings.description: Each frontend repo independently boots Supabase 3 times per CI trigger (integration-tests, codegen-check, e2e-axe). Each boot takes ~4 minutes, producing up to 36+ minutes of aggregate runner time per 3-repo push. No mechanism exists to share a running Supabase instance across sibling jobs in the same workflow.

tasks.description: Design a reusable CI job structure: a single 'supabase-boot' job that starts Supabase, extracts and caches keys, then is referenced as a service by all downstream jobs that depend on it.

Evaluate feasibility of installing a self-hosted GitHub Actions runner on the VM (14 GB RAM, 16 GB swap) to enable Docker daemon sharing, reducing per-boot time from ~4 min cold to ~30 sec warm sidecar start.
Alternatively, establish a persistent Supabase staging instance that CI jobs hit without booting. Document the tradeoffs vs. per-boot ephemeral approach.

findings.description: All four repos that use supabase/setup-cli@v1 specify 'version: latest'. The Supabase CLI is unpinned, meaning any upstream CLI breaking change (output format, command flags, schema diff format) silently propagates to all CI runs without warning.

tasks.description: Determine current Supabase CLI version in use and pin version: in all four ci.yml files to that specific version string (e.g. '2.x.y').
Add Renovate Bot configuration to auto-propose Supabase CLI version bumps as PRs, enabling controlled upgrades after human review.

findings.description: planner-frontend and workforce-frontend pass SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY from secrets.LOCAL_SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY — a static developer secret — rather than extracting the dynamically generated key from the ephemeral CI Supabase instance. If the local dev key differs from the CI instance key, service-role operations will silently fail or operate against an unintended instance.

tasks.description: Update planner-frontend ci.yml Start Supabase step to extract SERVICE_ROLE_KEY from 'supabase status -o env' and export it as SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, matching the preference-frontend pattern (grep '^SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=' | tr -d quotes).
Apply the same SERVICE_ROLE_KEY extraction fix to workforce-frontend ci.yml.
Remove LOCAL_SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY from GitHub Secrets for planner-frontend and workforce-frontend once dynamic extraction is confirmed working.

findings.description: No branch-matched ephemeral Supabase environment strategy exists. Every CI run cold-boots an independent instance. Two architectural patterns are viable: (A) isolated Docker networks per branch on a self-hosted runner (receptor-ci-<branch> network, fixed port 54321), or (B) a persistent staging instance with run-ID namespacing. Pattern A requires a self-hosted runner; Pattern B is feasible with GitHub-hosted runners but requires test data cleanup automation.

tasks.description: Document the two architectural options (A: Docker-network-per-branch on self-hosted runner; B: persistent staging with run-ID namespacing) in a new ADR at docs/infrastructure/environment/cicd-architecture.md in supabase-receptor.

If Option A chosen: extend setup.sh with a --ci flag that (1) skips interactive prompts, (2) accepts project-name as the Docker network/compose project, and (3) adds a --teardown flag for post-run cleanup. No external tunnel required for CI-internal access.
If Option B chosen: implement a pg_cron job on the staging instance that deletes orgs, users, and associated data where org_name LIKE '__test_%' AND created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'.

findings.description: setup.sh has no CI-native invocation mode. Calling it from CI requires interactive prompts for staging/prod tiers (read -s JWT_SECRET), uses PyYAML as an inline dependency, and has no idempotent fast-path. A CI-safe --ci flag is needed.

tasks.description: Add --ci flag to setup.sh that skips all interactive read prompts, fails loudly if required env vars are unset, and exits 0 only when the instance is confirmed healthy (docker compose ps check).
Add --teardown flag to RESET_setup.sh (or create a new teardown.sh) that is fully non-interactive, suitable for CI post-run cleanup.

findings.description: All frontend repos use GitHub-hosted ubuntu-latest runners. This prevents Docker daemon sharing and makes branch-matched ephemeral instances via Docker networks infeasible (Option A in ARCH-01). A self-hosted runner on the VM would enable shared Docker, warm Supabase sidecars (~30 sec vs ~4 min cold boot), and full network isolation between branches.

tasks.description: Evaluate self-hosted runner installation on the VM (14 GB RAM, 16 GB swap). Document memory requirements: a single Supabase stack uses ~3-4 GB, so the VM can support 2-3 concurrent branch test instances. Add this analysis to cicd-architecture.md.
If self-hosted runner is approved: install GitHub Actions runner on VM, configure as runs-on: [self-hosted, linux], and update all three frontend ci.yml files.

findings.description: Only a single dev environment is documented and instantiated. No test, staging, or prod tier is described, deployed, or referenced in any infrastructure documentation. docs/infrastructure/environment/supabase-self-hosted.md covers dev CLI workflow only.

tasks.description: Write docs/infrastructure/environment/environment-tiers.md defining the 4 tiers: dev (local), test (ephemeral CI), staging (persistent, staging-api-829c83.commonbond.au), and prod (api.commonbond.au). Include provisioning command, Cloudflare tunnel config, and data residency for each.
Provision the staging environment using setup.sh --env staging --project-name receptor-staging. Configure Cloudflare tunnel to staging-api-829c83.commonbond.au.

findings.description: planner-frontend ci.yml:180 uses 'npx graphql-codegen --config codegen.check.ts --check'. The --check flag performs in-memory comparison without running postcodegen hooks, producing false-positive drift failures when the committed file has been hook-patched (e.g. // @ts-nocheck prepended).

tasks.description: Replace '--check' with 'npm run codegen && git diff --exit-code' in planner-frontend codegen-check step, mirroring preference-frontend pattern. Add the specific error message annotation for developer guidance.

findings.description: workforce-frontend ci.yml:172 has the same --check defect as CGEN-01. Should be standardised to the regenerate + git diff pattern from preference-frontend.

tasks.description: Replace '--check' with 'npm run codegen && git diff --exit-code' in workforce-frontend codegen-check step.

findings.description: supabase-receptor ci.yml runs 'supabase start --ignore-health-check' with no --workdir flag. The working directory is assumed to be the repo root, which is fragile to future directory restructuring. Additionally, no key extraction step is present — if any future job needs the generated keys, there is no mechanism to retrieve them.

tasks.description: Add '--workdir supabase/' to the supabase start and supabase test db commands in supabase-receptor ci.yml to make the working directory explicit and refactor-safe.

findings.description: key-management.md references 'ANON_KEY' and 'SERVICE_ROLE_KEY' as the canonical keys to retrieve via 'npx supabase status', using the pre-migration key names. Since the Supabase key format migration, PUBLISHABLE_KEY is the new anon key and ANON_KEY is the legacy JWT. The doc is actively misleading for anyone following it.

tasks.description: Update key-management.md to reflect the new publishable key format: document PUBLISHABLE_KEY (sb_publishable_*) for REST/GraphQL client use, ANON_KEY (JWT, eyJ...) for signInWithPassword auth calls, and SERVICE_ROLE_KEY for server-side operations. Add a warning about the October 2025 migration deadline.

findings.description: setup.conf.example only contains localhost URLs. There are no template placeholders or commented sections for test (e.g. test-db.commonbond.internal), staging (staging-api-829c83.commonbond.au), or prod (api.commonbond.au) tier configurations.

tasks.description: Create setup.conf.staging.example and setup.conf.prod.example with correct public URLs, tunnel tokens (redacted), and non-localhost settings for each tier.

findings.description: RESET_setup.sh uses 'read -p' interactive confirmation at line 55. This prevents it from being called by a CI runner or automated promotion script without a TTY.

tasks.description: Add --force flag to RESET_setup.sh that skips the 'Are you sure?' prompt. Gate it behind a guard: if TARGET_ENV is staging or prod, require --force to be explicitly passed (refuse to teardown prod without explicit flag).

findings.description: No pg_cron job exists to purge stale test organisations (__test_* naming convention) from the shared staging/test instance. Test data accumulates indefinitely, creating data residency risk and quota consumption.

tasks.description: Create supabase/schemas/operations/test_cleanup.sql: a pg_cron job that deletes organisations WHERE name LIKE '__test_%' AND created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours', cascading to related user_orgs, job_lines, etc. via FK constraints.
Document the __test_{run-id} namespacing convention in the testing guide and require all integration test setup code to prefix created orgs with this pattern.

findings.description: All three frontend repos share the same TEST_ADMIN_EMAIL / TEST_WORKER_EMAIL GitHub Secrets with no run-ID namespace prefix. Concurrent CI runs from different branches on a shared Supabase instance could create or modify the same test organisation, causing flaky cross-branch test interference.

tasks.description: Define and document a __test_{GITHUB_RUN_ID} org naming convention. Update all three frontend repos' globalSetup to create test orgs using this prefix, and teardown in globalTeardown.

findings.description: seed_acacia.sql and test_user_credentials.json configure a single shared 'Acacia Enterprises' org. There is no mechanism for CI runs to work in isolated org namespaces — all runs compete for the same seed data.

tasks.description: Refactor seed.sql to seed a minimal base dataset only (no named org). Move the Acacia Enterprises dataset to seed_acacia.sql as a developer-only convenience seed. Update documentation to reflect this separation.

findings.description: No runbook exists for promoting a change through dev → test → staging → prod. setup.sh supports the --env flag but there is no documented promotion workflow, migration gate, or rollback procedure.

tasks.description: Write docs/operations/promotion-runbook.md describing the promotion workflow: (1) feature branch CI passes against ephemeral test instance, (2) merge to main triggers migration against staging, (3) manual approval gate for prod promotion, (4) rollback via RESET_setup.sh --env staging --force.

findings.description: key-management.md:86-88 has an open TODO block for integrating Bitwarden/Doppler CLI into the deployment workflow. This was identified in initial specifications but has never been actioned. No secrets vault is in place.

tasks.description: Either implement Doppler CLI integration in setup.sh (preferred — syncs across dev/test/staging/prod) or close the TODO by documenting a deliberate decision to use GitHub Secrets exclusively and update the key-management doc accordingly.

findings.description: match-backend integration tests (test_supabase_integration.py) are skipped in CI via pytest.mark.skipif when stub env vars are detected and are never run in CI. receptor-planner runs bare pytest with no path scoping — no unit/integration tier separation exists, meaning all tests run against stub URLs with misleading pass/fail results.

tasks.description: Add pytest path scoping to receptor-planner ci.yml: replace bare 'pytest' with 'pytest tests/unit/ -m unit' (or equivalent). Add a --co flag dryrun first to enumerate what's currently being collected.

Add an integration-tests job to match-backend ci.yml that boots a Supabase instance (using supabase/setup-cli@v1) and runs pytest allocator/tests/integration/ with real Supabase credentials extracted from supabase status -o env.

findings.description: Both backend repos set SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY to a hardcoded decoded JWT placeholder string (eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...) in their ci.yml. While intentionally non-functional, this leaks a JWT pattern into the repository that secret scanners (GitGuardian, GitHub secret scanning) may flag as a credential leak.

tasks.description: Replace the hardcoded JWT placeholder in both ci.yml files with a clearly non-JWT stub value such as 'stub-service-role-key-not-real' that will not trigger credential scanner heuristics.
Apply the same stub replacement to receptor-planner ci.yml.

findings.description: A k3s Kubernetes cluster on the available Windows 11 Pro machine (Intel i7-265KF 20-core, 32 GB DDR5, 1 TB NVMe, Hyper-V) would structurally resolve CICD-01, ARCH-01, ARCH-02, and ARCH-03. Namespace-per-branch CI with shared image cache reduces Supabase boot from ~4 min cold to ~30-60 sec warm. Traefik ingress enables branch-slug URLs without port juggling. Persistent dev/staging/prod namespaces with PVCs replace the current single-VM Docker Compose setup.

tasks.description: Design the k3s cluster topology: 3 Hyper-V VMs (control plane: 4 cores/6 GB, worker-1: 6 cores/10 GB for persistent envs, worker-2: 6 cores/10 GB for ephemeral branch CI). Document in docs/infrastructure/environment/kubernetes-cluster.md.
Provision a k3s cluster on the Windows 11 Pro machine using Hyper-V VMs running Ubuntu Server 24.04 LTS. Install k3s on the control plane and join the two worker nodes.

Deploy Supabase to the k3s cluster using the community Helm chart (https://github.com/supabase-community/supabase-kubernetes). Create namespaces: supabase-dev, supabase-staging, supabase-prod with separate PVCs per namespace.

Configure Traefik ingress rules to route <branch-slug>.ci.commonbond.local to the corresponding supabase-ci-<branch-slug> namespace. This replaces the port-juggling / Docker network isolation approach.
Install a GitHub Actions self-hosted runner as a Deployment in the control-plane namespace. Update all frontend ci.yml files to use runs-on: [self-hosted, linux, k3s] for Supabase-dependent jobs.

findings.description: No frontend or backend ci.yml file includes a top-level 'permissions:' block. Without explicit permissions, workflows inherit the default GITHUB_TOKEN permissions (read for most scopes, write for contents in some contexts). ISO 27001 A.8.3 (information access restriction) requires least-privilege for automated pipelines.

tasks.description: Add 'permissions: contents: read' at the top level of all 5 frontend/backend ci.yml files, then add specific write grants only where needed (e.g. pull-requests: write for PR comment steps if added in future).

Update SoA control 8.3 (Information access restriction) in docs/compliance/iso27001/operations/soa.md from 'Implemented' to reflect that GitHub Actions GITHUB_TOKEN now also enforces least-privilege via explicit permissions blocks. Update implementation notes to reference this audit finding.

findings.description: All CI files reference third-party Actions by tag (e.g. 'actions/checkout@v4', 'supabase/setup-cli@v1') rather than by commit SHA. A tag can be silently force-pushed to point to malicious code. ISO 27001 A.8.8 (management of technical vulnerabilities) requires awareness of supply-chain risks in build tooling.

tasks.description: Pin all third-party GitHub Actions to their full commit SHA (e.g. actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2). Configure Renovate Bot to auto-propose SHA bumps when new versions release.

Update SoA control 8.8 (Management of technical vulnerabilities) in docs/compliance/iso27001/operations/soa.md. Current status is 'Partial — Dependabot enabled; no formal vulnerability disclosure policy'. Add a note that CI Action SHA pinning + Renovate Bot extends supply-chain vulnerability management to the build toolchain.

findings.description: No ci.yml file in the ecosystem specifies a 'timeout-minutes:' on any job. If a Supabase boot hangs (a known failure mode with --ignore-health-check), the job will run until GitHub's hard 6-hour limit, consuming runner minutes and blocking the queue. Reference: GitHub docs recommend always setting job timeouts.

tasks.description: Add 'timeout-minutes: 20' to all Supabase-dependent jobs and 'timeout-minutes: 10' to pure unit/lint jobs across all 5 ci.yml files. Set a per-workflow timeout-minutes of 30 as a backstop.

findings.description: The ARCH-04 finding identifies a k3s Kubernetes cluster as the strategic infrastructure target, but no documentation exists for how to provision, update, or recover the cluster. Without runbooks, the cluster becomes a single-engineer knowledge silo.

tasks.description: Write docs/infrastructure/environment/kubernetes-cluster.md covering: cluster topology, VM provisioning steps (Hyper-V + Ubuntu Server 24.04 LTS), k3s installation commands, Helm chart deployment for Supabase, Traefik ingress rules for branch-slug routing, cert-manager DNS-01 challenge config, and disaster recovery procedure.

findings.description: No migration gate exists to prevent unreviewed schema changes from reaching the production Supabase instance. setup.sh allows running 'supabase db push' against prod without a mandatory human approval step or CI check. ISO 27001 A.8.32 (change management) requires documented approval for production changes.

tasks.description: Add a GitHub Actions workflow 'prod-deploy.yml' that triggers manually (workflow_dispatch) or on tag push to 'release/*'. The workflow runs 'supabase db diff --schema public' against prod, posts the diff as a workflow artifact for human review, and requires manual approval (GitHub Environment protection rule) before running 'supabase db push'.

Update SoA control 8.32 (Change management) in docs/compliance/iso27001/operations/soa.md from 'Implemented — all ISMS document changes via Git PR workflow; infrastructure changes reviewed informally' to 'Implemented' once prod-deploy.yml is active with GitHub Environment protection. Update the implementation notes to cite the prod-deploy.yml workflow as the production DB change gate.

Update Supabase audits table via REST API upsert to note that ENV-05 (production migration gate) addresses ISO 27001 A.8.32. This is a documentation-only update to the governance register — no schema change required.

clarifications.decision: Self-hosted Supabase instances are the target — not Supabase Cloud Pro. setup.sh is the canonical provisioning script. Any new environments must extend it, not replace it.
All environment tiers (dev, test, staging, prod) will run on the same VM for now. Architecture must be modular to allow future migration to separate hosts.
Staging instance will be assigned the Cloudflare tunnel subdomain staging-api-829c83.commonbond.au.

Two options to evaluate: (A) isolated Docker networks per branch using setup.sh --project-name receptor-ci-<branch> on a self-hosted runner; or (B) persistent staging with run-ID namespace prefix. Both should be documented as an ADR. Option A requires self-hosted runner co-location. A reverse proxy accepting feature-branch tags in the URL is also worth exploring.

All repos currently use GitHub-hosted ubuntu-latest runners. Installing a self-hosted runner on the VM is strongly recommended to unlock Docker daemon sharing and eliminate per-job 4-min Supabase cold boots. This is ARCH-03.
A pg_cron task should clean up stale __test_* orgs on the staging/test instance with a 24-hour TTL.

Supabase key format migration (JWT → sb_publishable_*/sb_secret_*) deadline was October 1 2025 (already passed). preference-frontend already exports dual keys. planner-frontend and workforce-frontend must be audited and fixed (KEY-01).

The available Windows 11 Pro workstation (Intel i7-265KF 20-core, 32 GB DDR5, 1 TB NVMe, RTX 5080, Hyper-V) is the proposed host for a k3s Kubernetes cluster. This is the strategic long-term solution for all CI/CD and environment tier hosting (ARCH-04). ARCH-04 + DOC-03 are the top implementation priority for this audit.

When SEC-01, SEC-02, ENV-05, and ARCH-04 are implemented, the corresponding ISO 27001 SoA entries (8.3, 8.8, 8.32, and 8.31) must be updated in docs/compliance/iso27001/operations/soa.md and the Supabase audits register. Each relevant finding has a dedicated compliance-update task.

implementationPhases.name: Strategic Priority: Kubernetes Cluster Planning & Infrastructure
implementationPhases.rationale: User-designated top priority for this audit. Design and provision the k3s cluster on Windows 11 Pro Hyper-V — this structurally resolves CICD-01, ARCH-01, ARCH-02, and ARCH-03 at the infrastructure level. Write the cluster runbook in parallel.

implementationPhases.name: Critical: Key Format & Service Role Fixes
implementationPhases.rationale: Fix the two security-class CI defects that cause silent auth failures. These are safe to implement immediately — they only change CI variable sourcing and can proceed in parallel with Phase 1 planning.

implementationPhases.name: High: CLI Pinning, Codegen Gate & Backend CI Cleanup
implementationPhases.rationale: Pin Supabase CLI version across all repos, standardise the codegen gate to the git-diff pattern, fix receptor-planner bare pytest, and replace hardcoded JWT stubs and add job timeouts.

implementationPhases.name: High: CI Security & Supply-Chain Hardening
implementationPhases.rationale: Add explicit GITHUB_TOKEN permission blocks and pin all third-party Actions to commit SHAs. Update SoA controls 8.3 and 8.8 upon completion.

implementationPhases.name: High: Environment Tiers & Documentation
implementationPhases.rationale: Provision staging, document all 4 environment tiers, write the promotion runbook, update key management docs. Strategic prerequisites before implementing CI architecture changes in Phase 6.

implementationPhases.name: High: CI Architecture & Test/Staging Isolation
implementationPhases.rationale: Design and implement the branch-matched CI architecture (ADR + runner decision), add CI mode to setup.sh, implement test data isolation and pg_cron cleanup. May be superseded by Phase 1 k8s namespace-per-branch approach.

implementationPhases.name: Medium: Production Gate & Compliance Records
implementationPhases.rationale: Add the prod migration gate workflow (ISO 27001 A.8.32) and update SoA upon completion. Update Supabase governance register.

implementationPhases.name: Low: Job Consolidation & Secrets Vault
implementationPhases.rationale: Reduce Supabase boots per CI run once architecture decisions from phases 1 and 6 are finalised, and resolve the open secrets vault TODO.
